calendar.matters:
DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Apr-28-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Petitioner REBECCA (BECKY) STEINBRUNER (Tel: 831-685-2915) in pro per;
Atty. JOEL S. JACOBS, Esq. of the California Attorney General's Office (Tel: 510-879-0279) for Respondent California Coastal Commission;
Atty. SARAH E. OWSOWITZ, Esq. of Best Best & Krieger LLC (Tel: 925-977-3300) for Respondent Suquel Creek Water District.

HEARING RE: RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA COASTAL COMMISSION DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Respondent California Coastal Commission's demurrer to the petition for writ of mandate is sustained without leave to amend. The petition for writ of mandate in this case challenges the Coastal Commission's March 11, 2020 approval of a permit for the PureWater Soquel Project, a water recycling and groundwater replenishment project, including development of a new tertiary water treatment facility in the City of Santa Cruz. The petition was filed on June 11, 2020. After extensively meeting and conferring over a period of months with petitioner, who repeatedly requested respondent to delay filing a demurrer and stated that she intended to file an amended petition, respondent filed and served its demurrer on March 17, 2021, and set it for hearing on April 14, 2021. That hearing date was continued to April 28, 2021 by agreement of the parties; as a result, petitioner's opposition to the demurrer was due to be filed no later than April 15, 2021, nine court days before the continued hearing date. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1005(b).) Petitioner did not file a timely opposition to the demurrer. Her late-filed "response" was filed on April 23, 2021, just three court days before the continued hearing; it is unclear whether Petitioner served it on respondent. Although Plaintiff is self-represented, that does not relieve her from the obligation to follow the rules of procedure or to act with reasonable diligence. (Burnete v. La Casa Dana Apartments (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 1262, 1267 ["the in propria persona litigant is held to the same restrictive rules of procedure as an attorney"].) That is particularly true here, where petitioner seeks to raise challenges to a significant land use project that was approved over one year ago, yet has failed to act with reasonable diligence, including failing timely to request a hearing in writing on her CEQA claim, which renders it subject to mandatory dismissal. (Pub. Res. Code sec 21167.4(k) [petitioner in CEQA action must request hearing "within 90 days from the date of filing the petition or shall be subject to dismissal on the court's own motion or on the motion of any party interested in the action or proceeding"]; Fiorentino v. City of Fresno (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 596, 603.) In any event, even if the Court were inclined to consider Petitioner's late-filed response, it does not present any pertinent authorities or argument, but merely asserts that Petitioner seeks leave to amend, without explaining what amendments she proposes to make or showing a reasonable likelihood that they could overcome the dispositive grounds set forth in respondent's demurrer. Accordingly, the demurrer to the petition is sustained without leave to amend.

Counsel for the respondent is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
